EXP-287 written: single sentence inserted into WIN-017.detail_evidence between "all methods agree" cross-validation clause and "Spacecraft-based measurements" sentence; flags spectroscopic-class distance estimates as the independence axis that does not flow through period-luminosity calibration of nearby parallaxes (Cepheid + RR Lyrae PL relations are calibrated using parallaxes of nearby standards — Riess et al. Gaia DR3 zero-point work — so "all methods agree" overstated cleanness of cross-check for those two methods; spectroscopic-class distances scale with apparent magnitude via a fixed M_V from spectral classification, independent of period-luminosity calibration). detail_evidence grew 204->263 words; no other WIN-017 field touched; no data values / counts / classifications affected. Three anticipated objections answered (spectroscopic-parallax M_V zero-pointing also uses parallax → M_V zero-pointing distinct from distance scaling; "most 1.8B Gaia stars are direct-parallax, why bother?" → defender-objection close-out, not evidence pillar; "no specific correlation paper cited" → conceptual independence-axis flag, future expansion can pin Geneva-Copenhagen / Strömgren-photometry compilation). EXP-287 marked complete in tracker; issue-proposal proposal-ISS-1847-resolution.json written for decider.
